title: Chief Executives and Managing Directors
rationale: Chief executives perform predominantly knowledge work — strategic planning, policy formulation, and organisational direction — all of which are digital and increasingly AI-augmentable. However, leadership, stakeholder relationships, and high-stakes decision-making provide some protection.

title: General Managers
rationale: General managers coordinate operations through digital communication, planning, and analysis. AI can assist with reporting, forecasting, and operational decisions, but human judgement and leadership remain essential.

title: Legislators
rationale: Legislators require public trust, political judgement, and constituency relationships. While policy analysis can be AI-assisted, the core political and representational work requires human presence and judgement.

title: Aquaculture Farmers
rationale: Aquaculture farming involves hands-on management of fish stocks, water quality, and physical infrastructure in variable environments. AI may assist with monitoring but cannot replace physical farm work.

title: Crop Farmers
rationale: Crop farming is primarily physical outdoor work — planting, tending, harvesting. Precision agriculture and AI monitoring are growing, but the core manual and environmental work remains human-dependent.

title: Livestock Farmers
rationale: Livestock farming requires physical presence with animals, hands-on animal husbandry, and work in unpredictable outdoor environments. AI has minimal impact on core duties.

title: Mixed Crop and Livestock Farmers
rationale: Mixed farming combines crop and livestock work, both highly physical. AI may help with farm management software but doesn't touch the core hands-on work.

title: Advertising, Public Relations and Sales Manager
rationale: Advertising and sales management involves strategy, campaign planning, and market analysis — all increasingly AI-augmentable. AI can generate content, analyse markets, and optimise campaigns, though client relationships remain important.

title: Corporate Services Managers
rationale: Corporate services management is predominantly knowledge work involving planning, compliance, and administration — areas where AI tools are already proving highly capable.

title: Finance Managers
rationale: Finance management involves financial analysis, budgeting, forecasting, and reporting — all digital tasks where AI excels. Human oversight and strategic judgement remain important.

title: Human Resource Managers
rationale: HR management combines strategic planning and policy development (highly AI-augmentable) with interpersonal elements like employee relations and leadership that provide some protection.

title: Policy and Planning Managers
rationale: Policy and planning managers do predominantly digital knowledge work — research, analysis, policy drafting — all areas where AI is rapidly improving. Strategic judgement and stakeholder management offer some insulation.

title: Research and Development Managers
rationale: R&D management involves coordinating research agendas and innovation strategies. While AI can accelerate research itself, directing and prioritising research still requires human expertise.

title: Construction Managers
rationale: Construction managers split time between on-site supervision (physical presence required) and planning/scheduling/documentation (increasingly AI-augmentable). The physical site presence component provides protection.

title: Engineering Managers
rationale: Engineering managers oversee technical projects with significant digital planning and analysis components. Site visits and technical oversight provide some physical presence requirements.

title: Importers, Exporters and Wholesalers
rationale: Import/export and wholesale management involves supply chain coordination, market analysis, and logistics — increasingly AI-optimisable — though relationship management and negotiation remain human skills.

title: Manufacturers
rationale: Manufacturing managers oversee physical production processes while also handling planning and coordination. The physical plant presence requirement provides moderate protection.

title: Production Managers
rationale: Production managers need physical presence on production floors while also doing planning and scheduling that AI can assist with. The split between physical and digital work provides moderate exposure.

title: Supply, Distribution and Procurement Managers
rationale: Supply chain and procurement management is heavily data-driven — demand forecasting, logistics optimisation, and vendor analysis are all areas where AI excels.

title: Child Care Centre Managers
rationale: Child care centre management combines administrative tasks (AI-augmentable) with hands-on supervision and interpersonal care of children. The caring and physical presence components provide significant protection.

title: Health and Welfare Services Managers
rationale: Health and welfare services management involves both administrative planning (AI-augmentable) and direct service oversight requiring human empathy and judgement.

title: School Principals
rationale: School principals combine educational leadership and community engagement (requiring human presence) with administrative tasks that AI can increasingly handle.

title: Other Education Managers
rationale: Education managers handle curriculum planning, policy development, and institutional administration — predominantly digital knowledge work with some need for interpersonal leadership.

title: ICT Managers
rationale: ICT managers work entirely in digital domains — planning technology strategy, overseeing systems, and managing technical teams. AI is already transforming how this work is done.

title: Commissioned Officers (Management)
rationale: Military officers require physical presence, leadership under pressure, and real-time decision-making in unpredictable environments. While AI assists with intelligence and planning, core military leadership is human.

title: Senior Non-Commissioned Defence Force Officers
rationale: Senior NCOs operate in physical military environments requiring hands-on leadership, training, and operational execution. AI has limited impact on core duties.

title: Other Specialist Managers
rationale: Other specialist managers typically perform knowledge work — analysis, planning, and coordination — with varying degrees of physical presence requirements.

title: Cafe and Restaurant Managers
rationale: Cafe and restaurant managers split between on-floor hospitality (physical presence, customer interaction) and administration (rostering, ordering, finances) where AI can help.

title: Caravan Park and Camping Ground Managers
rationale: Caravan park and camping ground managers need constant physical presence for site maintenance, guest services, and outdoor facility management.

title: Hotel and Motel Managers
rationale: Hotel managers combine guest-facing hospitality (physical presence) with operational management (increasingly AI-augmentable through revenue management, booking systems, etc.).

title: Licensed Club Managers
rationale: Licensed club management requires physical presence for venue operations and patron interaction, though administrative and financial tasks can be AI-assisted.

title: Other Accommodation and Hospitality Managers
rationale: Accommodation and hospitality managers need physical presence for guest services while benefiting from AI in administrative and operational tasks.

title: Retail Managers
rationale: Retail managers combine floor management and customer service (physical) with inventory, staffing, and merchandising analysis (increasingly AI-driven).

title: Amusement, Fitness and Sports Centre Managers
rationale: Fitness and sports centre managers require physical presence for facility oversight, member interaction, and operations management in physical environments.

title: Call, Contact Centre and Customer Service Managers
rationale: Call centre management is heavily data-driven — workforce scheduling, call routing, quality monitoring, and customer analytics. AI is already automating many of the underlying call centre functions.

title: Conference and Event Organisers
rationale: Conference and event organisers do significant digital planning and coordination, though event execution requires physical presence and real-time problem-solving.

title: Transport Services Managers
rationale: Transport services managers combine logistics coordination (highly AI-augmentable) with physical fleet and personnel oversight.

title: Other Hospitality, Retail and Service Managers
rationale: Other hospitality and service managers typically require physical presence while also performing administrative tasks that AI can assist with.

title: Actors, Dancers and Other Entertainers
rationale: Actors, dancers, and entertainers perform live physical/creative work requiring human presence, emotional expression, and audience connection. AI-generated content is emerging but live performance remains distinctly human.

title: Music Professionals
rationale: Music professionals create and perform through physical instruments and voice. While AI can generate music, live performance, teaching, and creative collaboration remain human activities.

title: Photographers
rationale: Photography involves both creative vision and technical execution. AI image generation is advancing rapidly, though event/portrait photography requiring physical presence has more protection.

title: Visual Arts and Crafts Professionals
rationale: Visual artists and craftspeople create physical works requiring manual skill and creative vision. While AI can generate digital art, physical craftsmanship remains human.

title: Artistic Directors, Media Producers and Presenters
rationale: Media producers and presenters combine creative direction (increasingly AI-augmentable) with on-screen/on-air presence (human-dependent). The digital content creation side is highly exposed.

title: Authors, and Book and Script Editors
rationale: Authors, book editors, and script editors work entirely in digital text — AI can already draft, edit, and generate written content at scale. This is one of the most exposed professional occupations.

title: Film, Television, Radio and Stage Directors
rationale: Film and stage directors require creative vision, leadership, and on-set presence. While AI assists with pre-production and post-production, live direction remains human.

title: Journalists and Other Writers
rationale: Journalists and writers produce digital text content — reporting, analysis, opinion. AI can already generate news articles, summaries, and written content, making this highly exposed.

title: Accountants
rationale: Accountants perform predominantly digital work — financial analysis, compliance, tax preparation, and reporting. AI and automation are already transforming accounting, with routine tasks being automated at scale.

title: Auditors, Company Secretaries and Corporate Treasurers
rationale: Auditors and company secretaries perform digital analysis, compliance checking, and documentation — all areas where AI is rapidly improving. Pattern detection in auditing is a natural AI application.

title: Financial Brokers
rationale: Financial brokers perform market analysis, client advisory, and transaction execution — all increasingly AI-augmentable. Relationship management provides some protection.

title: Financial Dealers
rationale: Financial dealers work in fully digital environments — trading, market analysis, risk assessment. Algorithmic and AI-driven trading is already dominant in many markets.

title: Financial Investment Advisers and Managers
rationale: Financial investment advisers perform portfolio analysis, risk assessment, and market research — all digital tasks where AI excels. Robo-advisors are already disrupting this space.

title: Human Resource Professionals
rationale: HR professionals handle recruitment, performance management, and workforce planning — increasingly AI-augmented through screening tools, analytics, and automated processes. Interpersonal elements provide some protection.

title: ICT Trainers
rationale: ICT trainers deliver training in digital domains. AI-generated tutorials, personalised learning, and interactive systems are rapidly emerging as alternatives.

title: Training and Development Professionals
rationale: Training and development professionals design and deliver programs — content creation is highly AI-augmentable, though in-person facilitation and needs assessment offer some protection.

title: Actuaries, Mathematicians and Statisticians
rationale: Actuaries, mathematicians, and statisticians perform quantitative analysis, modelling, and risk assessment — all digital, computational tasks where AI excels.

title: Archivists, Curators and Records Managers
rationale: Archivists and curators combine digital cataloguing and research (AI-augmentable) with physical collection management and exhibition work (requiring human presence and judgement).

title: Economists
rationale: Economists perform research, data analysis, modelling, and policy writing — all digital knowledge work where AI is increasingly capable.

title: Intelligence and Policy Analysts
rationale: Intelligence and policy analysts perform research, data analysis, and report writing — predominantly digital work where AI can process information and generate insights at scale.

title: Land Economists and Valuers
rationale: Land economists and valuers combine data analysis (AI-augmentable) with physical property inspections and local market knowledge requiring human presence.

title: Librarians
rationale: Librarians perform information management, research assistance, and cataloguing — increasingly automated through digital systems and AI search capabilities. Community engagement aspects provide some protection.

title: Management and Organisation Analysts
rationale: Management and organisation analysts perform consulting, process analysis, and report writing — predominantly digital knowledge work highly suited to AI augmentation.

title: Other Information and Organisation Professionals
rationale: Other information and organisation professionals typically perform digital knowledge work — research, analysis, and information management — with varying AI exposure.

title: Advertising and Marketing Professionals
rationale: Advertising and marketing professionals create campaigns, analyse markets, and produce content — all areas where AI is rapidly advancing in content generation, targeting, and analytics.

title: ICT Sales Professionals
rationale: ICT sales professionals combine technical knowledge with sales relationships. AI assists with technical proposals and market analysis, but relationship-building provides protection.

title: Public Relations Professionals
rationale: Public relations professionals create communications, manage media, and craft messaging — predominantly digital content work where AI can generate press releases, analyse sentiment, and manage responses.

title: Technical Sales Representatives
rationale: Technical sales representatives combine product knowledge with in-person client relationships and demonstrations. The relationship and physical demonstration aspects provide moderate protection.

title: Air Transport Professionals
rationale: Air transport professionals (pilots) operate complex machinery requiring physical presence, real-time decision-making, and regulatory compliance. While autopilot exists, human oversight remains critical for safety.

title: Marine Transport Professionals
rationale: Marine transport professionals operate vessels requiring physical presence, navigation skills, and real-time environmental response. Automation assists but cannot replace human presence.

title: Architects and Landscape Architects
rationale: Architects combine creative design (increasingly AI-augmentable through generative design) with client relationships and site work. The design component is highly digital and exposed.

title: Surveyors and Spatial Scientists
rationale: Surveyors combine fieldwork (physical measurement, site visits) with digital analysis and mapping. The physical component provides moderate protection.

title: Fashion, Industrial and Jewellery Designers
rationale: Fashion, industrial, and jewellery designers create designs — an increasingly AI-augmentable creative process, especially for digital design tools. Physical prototyping provides some protection.

title: Graphic and Web Designers, and Illustrators
rationale: Graphic and web designers work entirely in digital domains — visual design, layout, web interfaces. AI image generation and design tools are advancing extremely rapidly.

title: Interior Designers
rationale: Interior designers combine digital design work (AI-augmentable) with client consultations and physical site assessments. The in-person and spatial components provide moderate protection.

title: Urban and Regional Planners
rationale: Urban and regional planners combine data analysis and policy writing (AI-augmentable) with community consultation and site assessment (requiring human presence).

title: Chemical and Materials Engineers
rationale: Chemical engineers combine technical analysis and simulation (AI-augmentable) with laboratory and plant-based work requiring physical presence.

title: Civil Engineering Professionals
rationale: Civil engineering involves design calculations and documentation (increasingly AI-augmentable) alongside site inspections and project oversight requiring physical presence.

title: Electrical Engineers
rationale: Electrical engineers combine digital design and analysis (AI-augmentable) with physical installation oversight and testing.

title: Electronics Engineers
rationale: Electronics engineers work significantly in digital design, simulation, and testing — areas where AI is increasingly capable. Physical prototyping provides some protection.

title: Industrial, Mechanical and Production Engineers
rationale: Industrial and mechanical engineers combine design and analysis (AI-augmentable) with physical manufacturing oversight and testing.

title: Mining Engineers
rationale: Mining engineers work in physical mining environments requiring on-site presence, though planning and analysis components are increasingly digital and AI-augmentable.

title: Other Engineering Professionals
rationale: Other engineering professionals typically combine digital analysis and design (AI-augmentable) with varying degrees of physical site work.

title: Agricultural and Forestry Scientists
rationale: Agricultural scientists combine laboratory and field research (physical) with data analysis and report writing (AI-augmentable).

title: Chemists, and Food and Wine Scientists
rationale: Chemists and food scientists split between laboratory work (physical) and data analysis, research, and reporting (increasingly AI-augmentable).

title: Environmental Scientists
rationale: Environmental scientists combine fieldwork and sampling (physical) with data analysis, modelling, and report writing (AI-augmentable).

title: Geologists, Geophysicists and Hydrogeologists
rationale: Geologists combine extensive fieldwork (physical — rock sampling, site assessment) with data interpretation and modelling (AI-augmentable).

title: Life Scientists
rationale: Life scientists combine laboratory research (physical) with data analysis, literature review, and publication (increasingly AI-augmentable).

title: Medical Laboratory Scientists
rationale: Medical laboratory scientists perform hands-on laboratory testing and analysis. While AI assists with interpretation, the physical sample handling and testing requires human presence.

title: Veterinarians
rationale: Veterinarians perform hands-on animal examination, surgery, and treatment requiring physical presence and manual skill. AI may assist with diagnostics but cannot replace hands-on care.

title: Other Natural and Physical Science Professionals
rationale: Other science professionals typically combine physical research with digital analysis and reporting.

title: Early Childhood (Pre-primary School) Teachers
rationale: Early childhood teachers provide hands-on care, supervision, and education for young children. The interpersonal, caring, and physical presence requirements provide strong protection.

title: Primary School Teachers
rationale: Primary school teachers deliver face-to-face education, manage classrooms, and provide pastoral care. AI can augment lesson planning and assessment, but in-person teaching and child development support remain essential.

title: Middle School Teachers
rationale: Middle school teachers deliver face-to-face education with increasing subject specialisation. AI assists with content and assessment, but classroom management and mentoring require human presence.

title: Secondary School Teachers
rationale: Secondary school teachers deliver specialised subject education face-to-face. AI augments content delivery and assessment, but classroom interaction and student mentoring remain human.

title: Special Education Teachers
rationale: Special education teachers provide individualised, often physical support for students with disabilities. The high interpersonal and adaptive care requirements provide strong protection.

title: University Lecturers and Tutors
rationale: University lecturers and tutors deliver knowledge-based education and conduct research — both increasingly AI-augmentable. Content delivery and research writing are highly digital, though mentoring and lab supervision are less exposed.

title: Vocational Education Teachers
rationale: Vocational education teachers combine hands-on practical instruction (protected) with theory delivery and assessment (AI-augmentable).

title: Education Advisers and Reviewers
rationale: Education advisers perform curriculum development, policy analysis, and programme review — predominantly digital knowledge work that AI can significantly augment.

title: Private Tutors and Teachers
rationale: Private tutors provide personalised one-on-one instruction. AI tutoring systems are advancing but many students value human interaction. Exposure varies by subject.

title: Teachers of English to Speakers of Other Languages
rationale: TESOL teachers provide face-to-face language instruction with cultural and interpersonal elements. AI language tools are advancing rapidly but in-person teaching retains value.

title: Nutrition Professionals
rationale: Nutrition professionals combine scientific knowledge work (AI-augmentable) with client consultations and personalised health assessments requiring human interaction.

title: Medical Imaging Professionals
rationale: Medical imaging professionals operate complex equipment and interpret results. AI is advancing in image interpretation, but equipment operation and patient interaction require human presence.

title: Occupational and Environmental Health Professionals
rationale: Occupational and environmental health professionals combine site inspections (physical) with risk analysis and report writing (AI-augmentable).

title: Optometrists and Orthoptists
rationale: Optometrists perform hands-on eye examinations and patient care. While AI assists with diagnostics, the physical examination and patient interaction require human presence.

title: Pharmacists
rationale: Pharmacists combine medication dispensing (increasingly automated) with patient counselling and clinical judgement that requires human interaction.

title: Other Health Diagnostic and Promotion Professionals
rationale: Other health diagnostic professionals combine hands-on patient assessment with analysis and reporting.

title: Chiropractors and Osteopaths
rationale: Chiropractors and osteopaths perform hands-on physical treatments requiring manual skill, patient assessment, and physical presence. AI has minimal impact on core treatment work.

title: Complementary Health Therapists
rationale: Complementary health therapists provide hands-on treatments (massage, acupuncture, etc.) requiring physical presence and manual skill.

title: Dental Practitioners
rationale: Dental practitioners perform intricate manual procedures in patients' mouths. While AI assists with diagnostics and treatment planning, the hands-on work is highly protected.

title: Occupational Therapists
rationale: Occupational therapists provide hands-on patient assessment and treatment, adapting environments and activities. The physical and interpersonal components provide strong protection.

title: Physiotherapists
rationale: Physiotherapists perform hands-on physical treatments, exercise prescription, and patient assessment. The manual therapy and physical presence requirements provide strong protection.

title: Podiatrists
rationale: Podiatrists perform hands-on foot examination and treatment. The physical nature of the work provides strong protection from AI disruption.

title: Audiologists and Speech Pathologists \ Therapists
rationale: Audiologists and speech pathologists combine diagnostic testing (partially AI-augmentable) with hands-on therapy and patient interaction requiring human empathy and skill.

title: General Practitioners and Resident Medical Officers
rationale: GPs perform physical examinations, patient consultations, and clinical decision-making. AI assists with diagnostics and record-keeping, but patient interaction and physical examination remain essential.

title: Anaesthetists
rationale: Anaesthetists perform critical hands-on medical procedures requiring real-time patient monitoring, physical presence, and rapid decision-making in high-stakes environments.

title: Specialist Physicians
rationale: Specialist physicians combine clinical expertise with patient examination and complex diagnostic reasoning. AI assists with image interpretation and literature review, but hands-on patient care remains essential.

title: Psychiatrists
rationale: Psychiatrists provide therapy and medication management requiring deep interpersonal skills, empathy, and clinical judgement. While AI chatbots emerge, the therapeutic relationship remains fundamentally human.

title: Surgeons
rationale: Surgeons perform complex manual procedures requiring precise physical skills, real-time decision-making, and years of hands-on training. Robotic surgery assists but doesn't replace the surgeon.

title: Other Medical Practitioners
rationale: Other medical practitioners perform hands-on patient care with varying degrees of diagnostic support from AI.

title: Midwives
rationale: Midwives provide hands-on childbirth support, patient care, and emotional support in highly variable physical situations. The caring and physical presence components provide strong protection.

title: Nurse Educators and Researchers
rationale: Nurse educators and researchers perform predominantly digital knowledge work — curriculum development, research, and publication — with some clinical teaching.

title: Nurse Managers
rationale: Nurse managers combine clinical oversight (requiring healthcare environment presence) with administrative and planning tasks (AI-augmentable).

title: Registered Nurses
rationale: Registered nurses provide hands-on patient care, medication administration, and clinical monitoring. While AI assists with documentation and monitoring, the physical caring work is strongly protected.

title: ICT Business and Systems Analysts
rationale: ICT business and systems analysts perform digital analysis, requirements gathering, and documentation. AI can increasingly analyse systems, generate specifications, and identify improvements.

title: Multimedia Specialists and Web Developers
rationale: Multimedia specialists and web developers work entirely in digital creation — websites, apps, multimedia content. AI code generation and design tools are advancing extremely rapidly.

title: Software and Applications Programmers
rationale: Software programmers work entirely in digital domains — writing, testing, and maintaining code. AI coding assistants are already transforming software development and will continue to do so.

title: Database and Systems Administrators, and ICT Security Specialists
rationale: Database administrators and ICT security specialists work entirely in digital infrastructure. AI is increasingly capable of monitoring, managing, and securing digital systems.

title: Computer Network Professionals
rationale: Computer network professionals manage digital infrastructure. While physical hardware work provides some protection, network design, monitoring, and troubleshooting are increasingly AI-augmented.

title: ICT Support and Test Engineers
rationale: ICT support and test engineers perform digital testing and troubleshooting. AI-powered testing tools and automated diagnostics are rapidly advancing.

title: Telecommunications Engineering Professionals
rationale: Telecommunications engineers combine digital design and analysis with physical infrastructure work. The physical component provides moderate protection.

title: Barristers
rationale: Barristers perform legal research, argument preparation, and advocacy — largely digital knowledge work. AI can assist with research and drafting, though courtroom advocacy requires human presence.

title: Judicial and Other Legal Professionals
rationale: Judicial and legal professionals perform legal analysis, decision-making, and case management — increasingly AI-augmentable, though judicial authority and human judgement remain essential.

title: Solicitors
rationale: Solicitors perform legal research, document drafting, contract review, and compliance work — predominantly digital tasks where AI is already making significant inroads.

title: Counsellors
rationale: Counsellors provide therapeutic support through deep interpersonal connection, empathy, and emotional intelligence. While AI chatbots are emerging, the human therapeutic relationship is difficult to replicate.

title: Ministers of Religion
rationale: Ministers of religion provide spiritual guidance, community leadership, and pastoral care requiring deep personal connection and physical presence in communities.

title: Psychologists and Psychotherapists
rationale: Psychologists provide assessment, therapy, and clinical services requiring interpersonal skills, empathy, and clinical judgement. AI tools assist but cannot replace the therapeutic relationship.

title: Social Professionals
rationale: Social professionals combine community engagement and relationship work (protected) with research, policy analysis, and documentation (AI-augmentable).

title: Social Workers
rationale: Social workers provide direct client support, advocacy, and crisis intervention requiring empathy, relationship skills, and physical presence. Case documentation is AI-augmentable but core work is protected.

title: Welfare, Recreation and Community Arts Workers
rationale: Welfare and community arts workers engage directly with communities through interpersonal programs and physical activities. The human connection component provides strong protection.

title: Agricultural Technicians
rationale: Agricultural technicians perform hands-on fieldwork — soil testing, crop monitoring, and farm equipment operation. While AI assists with data analysis, physical work is protected.

title: Medical Technicians
rationale: Medical technicians perform hands-on laboratory and clinical procedures. AI assists with analysis but sample handling and equipment operation require human presence.

title: Primary Products Inspectors
rationale: Primary products inspectors perform physical inspections of food, livestock, and agricultural products requiring hands-on assessment and presence.

title: Science Technicians
rationale: Science technicians perform laboratory procedures and equipment operation. AI assists with data analysis but hands-on work is protected.

title: Architectural, Building and Surveying Technicians
rationale: Architectural and surveying technicians combine digital drafting and modelling (AI-augmentable) with site measurement and inspection (physical).

title: Civil Engineering Draftspersons and Technicians
rationale: Civil engineering draftspersons produce technical drawings and documentation — increasingly AI-augmentable through CAD automation and generative design.

title: Electrical Engineering Draftspersons, Technicians
rationale: Electrical engineering draftspersons create technical drawings and specifications — digital work that AI-powered design tools can increasingly assist with.

title: Electronic Engineering Draftspersons, Technicians
rationale: Electronic engineering draftspersons work primarily with digital design tools. AI is advancing in circuit design and layout automation.

title: Mechanical Engineering Draftspersons, Technicians
rationale: Mechanical engineering draftspersons produce technical drawings and specifications — digital work increasingly augmented by AI-powered CAD tools.

title: Safety Inspectors
rationale: Safety inspectors perform physical site inspections requiring presence, observation, and hands-on assessment of workplace conditions.

title: Other Building and Engineering Technicians
rationale: Other building and engineering technicians combine technical documentation (AI-augmentable) with physical site work.

title: ICT Support Technicians
rationale: ICT support technicians troubleshoot and maintain computer systems. AI-powered diagnostics and remote management tools are increasingly capable, though physical hardware work provides some protection.

title: Telecommunications Technical Specialists
rationale: Telecommunications technical specialists combine physical equipment installation and maintenance with digital configuration and troubleshooting.

title: Automotive Electricians
rationale: Automotive electricians perform hands-on diagnostic and repair work on vehicle electrical systems. The physical, dexterity-requiring work is highly protected from AI.

title: Motor Mechanics
rationale: Motor mechanics perform hands-on vehicle diagnosis and repair requiring physical skill and problem-solving in variable conditions. AI assists with diagnostics but not physical repair.

title: Metal Casting, Forging and Finishing Trades
rationale: Metal casting, forging, and finishing involves intensive hands-on work with heavy materials in industrial environments. AI has minimal impact on core physical tasks.

title: Sheetmetal Trades Workers
rationale: Sheetmetal trades involve hands-on cutting, bending, and fabricating metal components. The physical skill requirements provide very strong protection.

title: Structural Steel and Welding Trades Workers
rationale: Structural steel and welding trades require hands-on welding, cutting, and fabrication in variable physical environments. Highly protected from AI disruption.

title: Aircraft Maintenance Engineers
rationale: Aircraft maintenance engineers perform hands-on inspection, repair, and maintenance of aircraft. Safety-critical physical work with strong regulatory requirements.

title: Metal Fitters and Machinists
rationale: Metal fitters and machinists perform hands-on fitting, turning, and machining operations. While CNC automation exists, skilled manual work remains essential.

title: Precision Metal Trades Workers
rationale: Precision metal trades require fine manual skills for instrument making and repair. The exacting physical nature provides strong protection.

title: Toolmakers and Engineering Patternmakers
rationale: Toolmakers and patternmakers perform precise manual fabrication. While CNC assists, the skilled handwork and problem-solving remain human.

title: Panelbeaters
rationale: Panelbeaters perform hands-on vehicle body repair requiring manual skill, physical strength, and spatial judgement. Highly protected from AI.

title: Vehicle Body Builders and Trimmers
rationale: Vehicle body builders and trimmers perform hands-on fabrication and fitting work requiring physical skill.

title: Vehicle Painters
rationale: Vehicle painters perform physical spray painting requiring manual skill, surface preparation, and environmental control. Robots assist in factories but not in repair shops.

title: Bricklayers and Stonemasons
rationale: Bricklaying and stonemasonry are hands-on construction trades requiring physical strength, spatial awareness, and manual precision. Highly protected.

title: Carpenters and Joiners
rationale: Carpentry and joinery involve hands-on construction work requiring manual skill, measurement, and physical labour on varied sites.

title: Floor Finishers
rationale: Floor finishing involves hands-on surface preparation, material laying, and finishing. Physical trade work with strong protection.

title: Painting Trades Workers
rationale: Painting trades involve physical surface preparation and paint application in varied environments. Manual skill and site presence required.

title: Glaziers
rationale: Glaziers install and repair glass in buildings — hands-on work requiring physical handling of heavy, fragile materials.

title: Plasterers
rationale: Plasterers apply and finish plaster and render — hands-on construction work requiring physical skill.

title: Roof Tilers
rationale: Roof tiling is physical outdoor work at heights, requiring manual skill and weather adaptation. Very highly protected.

title: Wall and Floor Tilers
rationale: Wall and floor tiling involves hands-on measurement, cutting, and laying of tiles. Physical trade work.

title: Plumbers
rationale: Plumbers install and maintain water and gas systems. Hands-on physical work in varied building environments. AI may assist with diagnostics but not physical pipe fitting.

title: Electricians
rationale: Electricians install and maintain electrical systems. Physical hands-on work requiring safety awareness and manual skill. AI assists with diagnostics but not installation.

title: Airconditioning and Refrigeration Mechanics
rationale: Airconditioning mechanics install and repair HVAC systems — hands-on physical work in varied environments.

title: Electrical Distribution Trades Workers
rationale: Electrical distribution trades workers maintain power networks — physical outdoor work at heights with safety-critical requirements.

title: Electronics Trades Workers
rationale: Electronics trades workers repair and maintain electronic equipment. Some diagnostic work is AI-augmentable, but hands-on repair requires physical skill.

title: Telecommunications Trades Workers
rationale: Telecommunications trades workers install and maintain physical network infrastructure — cabling, equipment, and outdoor installations.

title: Bakers and Pastrycooks
rationale: Bakers and pastrycooks perform hands-on food preparation requiring manual skill, timing, and sensory judgement (taste, texture, smell).

title: Butchers and Smallgoods Makers
rationale: Butchers perform hands-on meat cutting and processing requiring physical skill and food safety knowledge.

title: Chefs
rationale: Chefs prepare food in fast-paced kitchen environments requiring manual skill, creativity, and real-time coordination. The physical and creative elements provide strong protection.

title: Cooks
rationale: Cooks perform hands-on food preparation in commercial kitchens. Physical, time-pressured work with strong protection.

title: Animal Attendants and Trainers
rationale: Animal attendants work hands-on with animals — feeding, grooming, exercising, and monitoring health. Physical presence with unpredictable animals is essential.

title: Shearers
rationale: Shearing is intensive hands-on physical work with livestock in rural environments. Very highly protected from AI.

title: Veterinary Nurses
rationale: Veterinary nurses provide hands-on animal care, assisting with procedures and monitoring. Physical animal handling is essential, though record-keeping is AI-augmentable.

title: Florists
rationale: Florists create physical arrangements from natural materials requiring manual skill, creativity, and customer interaction.

title: Gardeners
rationale: Gardeners perform physical outdoor work — planting, pruning, mowing. Hands-on work in varied natural environments.

title: Greenkeepers
rationale: Greenkeepers maintain turf and grounds — physical outdoor work requiring hands-on care and environmental adaptation.

title: Nurserypersons
rationale: Nurserypersons propagate and maintain plants — hands-on horticultural work requiring physical presence and plant knowledge.

title: Hairdressers
rationale: Hairdressers perform hands-on hair cutting, styling, and treatment. Physical work requiring manual skill and client interaction. Strongly protected.

title: Print Finishers and Screen Printers
rationale: Print finishers operate machines and perform manual finishing tasks. Some automation exists but skilled manual work and machine operation remain essential.

title: Graphic Pre-press Trades Workers
rationale: Graphic pre-press workers prepare digital files for printing. This digital workflow is increasingly automated through AI-powered tools.

title: Printers
rationale: Printers operate complex printing machinery. While digital printing advances, machine operation and maintenance require physical presence.

title: Canvas and Leather Goods Makers
rationale: Canvas and leather goods makers perform hands-on fabrication requiring manual skill and craftwork.

title: Clothing Trades Workers
rationale: Clothing trades workers perform hands-on garment construction. While industrial sewing is partially automated, skilled handwork remains essential.

title: Upholsterers
rationale: Upholsterers perform hands-on furniture re-covering requiring manual skill and physical work.

title: Cabinetmakers
rationale: Cabinetmakers construct and install furniture and fittings. Hands-on woodworking combining machine operation with manual skill.

title: Wood Machinists and Other Wood Trades Workers
rationale: Wood machinists operate woodworking machinery. While CNC assists, machine setup and operation require physical presence.

title: Boat Builders and Shipwrights
rationale: Boat builders construct and repair vessels — hands-on physical work requiring diverse manual skills.

title: Chemical, Gas, Petroleum and Power Plant Operators
rationale: Plant operators monitor and control chemical, gas, and power plant processes. AI assists with monitoring but physical presence for safety and maintenance is essential.

title: Gallery, Library and Museum Technicians
rationale: Gallery, library, and museum technicians combine physical collection handling with digital cataloguing and exhibition support.

title: Jewellers
rationale: Jewellers perform intricate hands-on work with precious materials requiring fine manual skill. Creative and physical work with strong protection.

title: Performing Arts Technicians
rationale: Performing arts technicians operate physical equipment — lighting, sound, staging — requiring presence and real-time adaptation.

title: Signwriters
rationale: Signwriters create physical signs combining design (increasingly digital) with physical installation and fabrication.

title: Other Miscellaneous Technicians and Trades Workers
rationale: Other miscellaneous technicians and trades workers typically perform hands-on physical work with varying degrees of digital support.

title: Ambulance Officers and Paramedics
rationale: Ambulance officers and paramedics provide emergency medical care in unpredictable, high-pressure physical environments. Hands-on clinical skills and rapid decision-making are essential.

title: Dental Hygienists, Technicians and Therapists
rationale: Dental hygienists and technicians perform hands-on dental procedures and laboratory work. Physical patient contact and manual skill are essential.

title: Diversional Therapists
rationale: Diversional therapists engage clients in hands-on activities and personal interaction. The interpersonal and physical activity components provide strong protection.

title: Enrolled and Mothercraft Nurses
rationale: Enrolled nurses provide hands-on patient care including medication administration, wound care, and monitoring. Physical presence and caring skills are essential.

title: Indigenous Health Workers
rationale: Indigenous health workers provide culturally specific health services requiring community trust, cultural knowledge, and personal relationships.

title: Massage Therapists
rationale: Massage therapists provide hands-on physical treatments. The manual, tactile nature of the work provides very strong protection from AI.

title: Welfare Support Workers
rationale: Welfare support workers provide direct client support, advocacy, and practical assistance. The interpersonal and community-based work is strongly protected.

title: Child Carers
rationale: Child carers provide hands-on supervision, care, and education for young children. Physical presence, safety supervision, and emotional nurturing are essential human tasks.

title: Education Aides
rationale: Education aides support teachers with hands-on student assistance, classroom management, and individual student support.

title: Aged and Disabled Carers
rationale: Aged and disabled carers provide hands-on personal care — bathing, dressing, feeding, mobility assistance. Physical caring work with strong protection.

title: Dental Assistants
rationale: Dental assistants provide hands-on chairside assistance during dental procedures. Physical presence and manual dexterity are essential.

title: Nursing Support and Personal Care Workers
rationale: Nursing support workers provide hands-on patient care — personal hygiene, mobility, feeding. Physical caring work that is strongly protected.

title: Special Care Workers
rationale: Special care workers provide hands-on support for people with disabilities or challenging behaviours in residential and community settings.

title: Bar Attendants and Baristas
rationale: Bar attendants and baristas prepare drinks and serve customers. Physical food/beverage preparation and face-to-face customer interaction provide strong protection.

title: Cafe Workers
rationale: Cafe workers prepare food and serve customers. Hands-on food preparation and customer service in physical environments.

title: Gaming Workers
rationale: Gaming workers operate gaming machines and tables. Some tasks are automatable, but floor supervision and customer interaction require presence.

title: Hotel Service Managers
rationale: Hotel service managers combine guest-facing service (physical presence) with operational coordination (partially AI-augmentable).

title: Waiters
rationale: Waiters serve food and beverages, requiring physical presence, customer interaction, and real-time responsiveness.

title: Other Hospitality Workers
rationale: Other hospitality workers perform hands-on food service, cleaning, and customer interaction tasks.

title: Defence Force Members - Other Ranks
rationale: Defence force members perform physical military duties in varied and unpredictable environments. Hands-on skills and physical presence are essential.

title: Fire and Emergency Workers
rationale: Fire and emergency workers respond to emergencies requiring physical courage, hands-on rescue skills, and real-time decision-making in dangerous environments.

title: Police
rationale: Police officers perform patrol, investigation, and community engagement requiring physical presence, interpersonal skills, and real-time judgement. AI assists with data analysis but not frontline policing.

title: Prison Officers
rationale: Prison officers supervise inmates in custodial settings requiring physical presence, security awareness, and interpersonal management.

title: Security Officers and Guards
rationale: Security officers and guards provide physical security through presence, patrol, and access control. Human presence is the core function.

title: Beauty Therapists
rationale: Beauty therapists provide hands-on treatments — facials, waxing, skin care. Physical, manual work with strong protection.

title: Driving Instructors
rationale: Driving instructors teach in-vehicle — requiring physical presence, real-time instruction, and adaptation to traffic conditions.

title: Funeral Workers
rationale: Funeral workers provide physical services — body preparation, ceremony management, and family support requiring empathy and presence.

title: Gallery, Museum and Tour Guides
rationale: Gallery, museum, and tour guides provide in-person interpretation and engagement. While AI audio guides exist, human interaction and storytelling add value.

title: Personal Care Consultants
rationale: Personal care consultants provide beauty and skincare advice. Online AI recommendations are growing, but in-person consultations and product demonstrations remain valued.

title: Tourism and Travel Advisers
rationale: Tourism and travel advisers perform largely digital work — researching destinations, booking travel, and comparing options. Online booking platforms and AI assistants are rapidly displacing this role.

title: Travel Attendants
rationale: Travel attendants (flight attendants, etc.) provide in-flight/in-transit passenger service requiring physical presence, safety duties, and customer care.

title: Other Personal Service Workers
rationale: Other personal service workers typically provide hands-on services requiring physical presence and interpersonal interaction.

title: Fitness Instructors
rationale: Fitness instructors lead physical exercise sessions requiring demonstration, motivation, and real-time form correction. Physical presence and interpersonal energy are essential.

title: Outdoor Adventure Guides
rationale: Outdoor adventure guides lead physical activities in natural environments — unpredictable terrain, weather, and safety management. Very strongly protected.

title: Sports Coaches, Instructors and Officials
rationale: Sports coaches provide in-person physical training, technique instruction, and motivational leadership. The physical and interpersonal elements provide strong protection.

title: Sportspersons
rationale: Sportspersons compete physically in athletic events. The physical performance is inherently human and cannot be replaced by AI.

title: Contract, Program and Project Administrators
rationale: Contract and project administrators perform digital documentation, scheduling, tracking, and reporting. AI project management tools are rapidly advancing.

title: Office Managers
rationale: Office managers coordinate administrative functions — scheduling, correspondence, filing — predominantly digital tasks increasingly augmented by AI.

title: Practice Managers
rationale: Practice managers handle scheduling, billing, and administrative coordination — largely digital workflow management that AI can significantly augment.

title: Personal Assistants
rationale: Personal assistants manage calendars, correspondence, travel, and documents — predominantly digital tasks where AI assistants are rapidly improving.

title: Secretaries
rationale: Secretaries perform typing, correspondence, filing, and scheduling — routine digital tasks that AI can already handle at high quality.

title: General Clerks
rationale: General clerks perform routine administrative tasks — data entry, filing, correspondence, and record-keeping. These routine digital tasks are highly automatable.

title: Keyboard Operators
rationale: Keyboard operators perform data entry and typing — routine, fully digital tasks that AI can already do faster and more accurately.

title: Call or Contact Centre Workers
rationale: Call or contact centre workers handle customer queries by phone/chat. AI chatbots and voice systems are already replacing many of these functions.

title: Information Officers
rationale: Information officers provide information to the public — increasingly done through AI chatbots, automated systems, and self-service portals.

title: Receptionists
rationale: Receptionists greet visitors, manage calls, and coordinate bookings. Automated systems handle many of these functions, though some physical presence value remains.

title: Accounting Clerks
rationale: Accounting clerks perform routine financial data entry, invoicing, and reconciliation — digital tasks highly suited to AI automation.

title: Bookkeepers
rationale: Bookkeepers maintain financial records, process transactions, and reconcile accounts — routine digital tasks increasingly automated by accounting software and AI.

title: Payroll Clerks
rationale: Payroll clerks process payroll, calculate deductions, and maintain records — routine digital processing highly suited to automation.

title: Bank Workers
rationale: Bank workers process transactions, manage accounts, and handle customer queries — largely automated through digital banking and AI.

title: Credit and Loans Officers
rationale: Credit and loans officers assess applications and process loans. AI credit scoring and automated assessment are advancing, though complex cases require human judgement.

title: Insurance, Money Market and Statistical Clerks
rationale: Insurance, money market, and statistical clerks perform routine data processing and record-keeping in financial services — highly automatable digital tasks.

title: Betting Clerks
rationale: Betting clerks process bets and calculate payouts — routine digital transactions increasingly automated.

title: Couriers and Postal Deliverers
rationale: Couriers and postal deliverers perform physical delivery work requiring navigation, vehicle operation, and package handling. While drones and autonomous vehicles are emerging, human delivery remains dominant.

title: Filing and Registry Clerks
rationale: Filing and registry clerks organise and retrieve physical and digital records — routine tasks that digital systems and AI can largely automate.

title: Mail Sorters
rationale: Mail sorters sort and distribute mail — routine physical/digital sorting tasks increasingly automated.

title: Survey Interviewers
rationale: Survey interviewers collect data through structured questions — a task AI can perform through digital surveys, chatbots, and automated calling.

title: Switchboard Operators
rationale: Switchboard operators route telephone calls — a function almost entirely automated by modern phone systems and AI.

title: Other Clerical and Office Support Workers
rationale: Other clerical support workers perform routine digital administrative tasks — filing, data entry, correspondence — highly automatable.

title: Purchasing and Supply Logistics Clerks
rationale: Purchasing and supply clerks process orders, manage inventory, and coordinate logistics — increasingly automated through AI-powered procurement systems.

title: Transport and Despatch Clerks
rationale: Transport and despatch clerks coordinate shipments and logistics. While digital coordination is AI-augmentable, physical warehouse presence may be required.

title: Conveyancers and Legal Executives
rationale: Conveyancers and legal executives perform document preparation, searches, and legal administration — routine legal tasks where AI is already making inroads.

title: Court and Legal Clerks
rationale: Court and legal clerks manage case files, prepare documents, and maintain records — predominantly digital legal administration highly suited to AI.

title: Debt Collectors
rationale: Debt collectors contact debtors and negotiate payments. AI-powered automated calling and messaging systems are increasingly used, though complex negotiations remain human.

title: Human Resource Clerks
rationale: Human resource clerks process HR paperwork, maintain records, and handle routine queries — digital administrative tasks highly suited to AI automation.

title: Inspectors and Regulatory Officers
rationale: Inspectors and regulatory officers perform physical inspections and compliance checks requiring on-site presence. Report writing is AI-augmentable but physical inspection is protected.

title: Insurance Investigators, Loss Adjusters and Risk Surveyors
rationale: Insurance investigators combine field investigation (physical presence) with digital analysis and report writing.

title: Library Assistants
rationale: Library assistants manage collections, process loans, and assist patrons — tasks increasingly automated through digital library systems and AI search.

title: Other Miscellaneous Clerical and Administrative Workers
rationale: Other miscellaneous clerical workers perform routine digital administrative tasks.

title: Auctioneers, and Stock and Station Agents
rationale: Auctioneers and stock agents combine in-person auctioneering (requiring presence and performance) with market analysis and client relationships.

title: Insurance Agents
rationale: Insurance agents sell policies and provide advice — increasingly done through comparison websites, AI recommendations, and automated underwriting.

title: Sales Representatives
rationale: Sales representatives combine relationship building and in-person client visits with digital communication and proposal preparation (AI-augmentable).

title: Real Estate Sales Agents
rationale: Real estate agents combine property inspections and buyer/seller meetings (physical) with market analysis and marketing (increasingly AI-augmentable).

title: Sales Assistants (General)
rationale: Retail sales assistants provide in-store customer service, product advice, and transaction processing. Physical presence and interpersonal service remain important despite self-checkout growth.

title: ICT Sales Assistants
rationale: ICT sales assistants combine technical product knowledge with in-store customer service. Online shopping and AI recommendations reduce but don't eliminate the need.

title: Motor Vehicle and Vehicle Parts Salespersons
rationale: Motor vehicle salespersons provide in-person vehicle demonstrations, negotiations, and customer service. The high-value, physical product requires human interaction.

title: Pharmacy Sales Assistants
rationale: Pharmacy sales assistants provide in-person customer service and product advice in a health retail environment requiring human interaction.

title: Retail Supervisors
rationale: Retail supervisors oversee store operations and staff — requiring physical presence while administrative tasks are AI-augmentable.

title: Service Station Attendants
rationale: Service station attendants provide physical fuel service, shop sales, and site maintenance. Physical presence is essential.

title: Street Vendors and Related Salespersons
rationale: Street vendors operate in physical outdoor environments requiring presence, customer interaction, and manual food/goods handling.

title: Other Sales Assistants and Salespersons
rationale: Other sales assistants provide in-person customer service with varying degrees of digital support.

title: Checkout Operators and Office Cashiers
rationale: Checkout operators process transactions — increasingly automated through self-checkout and contactless payment. Some customer interaction value remains.

title: Models and Sales Demonstrators
rationale: Models and sales demonstrators provide physical product demonstrations and presentations requiring human presence.

title: Retail and Wool Buyers
rationale: Retail and wool buyers perform market analysis, price negotiation, and supply assessment — increasingly AI-augmentable through data analytics.

title: Telemarketers
rationale: Telemarketers make outbound sales calls — a function increasingly performed by AI-powered calling systems and chatbots.

title: Ticket Salespersons
rationale: Ticket salespersons sell tickets for events and transport — largely automated through online booking platforms.

title: Visual Merchandisers
rationale: Visual merchandisers design retail displays. AI-powered design tools are advancing, though physical installation requires presence.

title: Other Sales Support Workers
rationale: Other sales support workers perform various sales-related tasks with varying digital exposure.

title: Clay, Concrete, Glass and Stone Processing Machine Operators
rationale: Machine operators in manufacturing operate physical processing equipment requiring presence, monitoring, and manual adjustment.

title: Industrial Spraypainters
rationale: Industrial spraypainters operate painting equipment in manufacturing environments. Physical operation and quality control require presence.

title: Paper and Wood Processing Machine Operators
rationale: Paper and wood processing machine operators run physical production equipment requiring hands-on operation and monitoring.

title: Photographic Developers and Printers
rationale: Photographic developers and printers operate increasingly digital workflows. Traditional darkroom work is declining as digital processing and AI image tools advance.

title: Plastics and Rubber Production Machine Operators
rationale: Plastics and rubber machine operators run physical production equipment requiring hands-on operation, monitoring, and adjustment.

title: Sewing Machinists
rationale: Sewing machinists operate industrial sewing machines. Physical garment production requiring manual skill and machine operation.

title: Textile and Footwear Production Machine Operators
rationale: Textile and footwear machine operators run physical production equipment in manufacturing environments.

title: Other Machine Operators
rationale: Other machine operators run various physical production equipment requiring presence and manual operation.

title: Crane, Hoist and Lift Operators
rationale: Crane, hoist, and lift operators control heavy lifting equipment requiring real-time spatial judgement and safety awareness. Physical presence is essential.

title: Drillers, Miners and Shot Firers
rationale: Drillers, miners, and shot firers work in underground and surface mining environments requiring physical presence in challenging, dangerous conditions.

title: Engineering Production Workers
rationale: Engineering production workers operate machinery and perform assembly tasks. Some automation exists but human oversight and manual work remain essential.

title: Other Stationary Plant Operators
rationale: Other stationary plant operators monitor and control fixed industrial equipment requiring physical presence.

title: Agricultural, Forestry and Horticultural Plant Operators
rationale: Agricultural plant operators operate tractors and farm machinery in varied outdoor conditions. GPS and automation assist but physical operation remains essential.

title: Earthmoving Plant Operators
rationale: Earthmoving plant operators operate heavy equipment (excavators, bulldozers) in construction and mining. Physical operation in varied terrain requires human presence.

title: Forklift Drivers
rationale: Forklift drivers operate forklifts in warehouses and worksites. While warehouse automation grows, human forklift operation remains widespread.

title: Other Mobile Plant Operators
rationale: Other mobile plant operators control various mobile equipment requiring physical operation skills.

title: Automobile Drivers
rationale: Automobile drivers (taxi, rideshare) transport passengers. Autonomous vehicles are developing but remain limited; human driving is still dominant.

title: Bus and Coach Drivers
rationale: Bus and coach drivers transport passengers on fixed routes. While autonomous bus trials exist, human drivers remain essential for safety and passenger assistance.

title: Train and Tram Drivers
rationale: Train and tram drivers operate rail vehicles. Automated trains exist on some systems, but most still require human operators for safety.

title: Delivery Drivers
rationale: Delivery drivers transport goods. Autonomous delivery is emerging but human drivers dominate, especially for varied delivery environments.

title: Truck Drivers
rationale: Truck drivers transport goods over long distances. While autonomous trucking is developing, human drivers remain essential for complex road conditions and loading/unloading.

title: Storepersons
rationale: Storepersons receive, store, and dispatch goods in warehouses. Warehouse automation is growing but human workers remain essential for varied tasks.

title: Car Detailers
rationale: Car detailers perform hands-on vehicle cleaning and polishing. Manual physical work with strong protection.

title: Commercial Cleaners
rationale: Commercial cleaners clean buildings and facilities. Physical manual work requiring presence and adaptation to varied environments.

title: Domestic Cleaners
rationale: Domestic cleaners clean private homes. Physical work requiring manual skill and adaptation to varied environments.

title: Housekeepers
rationale: Housekeepers maintain cleanliness and order in accommodation. Physical work requiring presence and manual skills.

title: Laundry Workers
rationale: Laundry workers process garments and linens. Physical handling work, though some industrial laundry processes are automated.

title: Other Cleaners
rationale: Other cleaners perform various physical cleaning tasks. Manual work with strong protection.

title: Building and Plumbing Labourers
rationale: Building and plumbing labourers perform heavy physical construction work. Manual labour in varied site conditions.

title: Concreters
rationale: Concreters prepare and pour concrete. Heavy physical work requiring manual skill and site presence.

title: Fencers
rationale: Fencers install and repair fences. Physical outdoor work requiring manual skills.

title: Insulation and Home Improvement Installers
rationale: Insulation and home improvement installers perform hands-on installation work. Physical trade assistance.

title: Paving and Surfacing Labourers
rationale: Paving and surfacing labourers perform heavy physical road and path construction.

title: Railway Track Workers
rationale: Railway track workers maintain and construct rail infrastructure. Physical outdoor work in challenging conditions.

title: Structural Steel Construction Workers
rationale: Structural steel construction workers erect steel frameworks. Heavy physical work at heights.

title: Other Construction and Mining Labourers
rationale: Other construction and mining labourers perform heavy physical work in varied conditions.

title: Food and Drink Factory Workers
rationale: Food and drink factory workers perform hands-on food processing. Physical production work, though some factory processes are automated.

title: Meat Boners and Slicers, and Slaughterers
rationale: Meat boners and slicers perform hands-on meat cutting requiring physical skill and speed.

title: Meat, Poultry and Seafood Process Workers
rationale: Meat, poultry, and seafood process workers perform physical food processing tasks.

title: Packers
rationale: Packers manually package products for distribution. Physical handling work.

title: Product Assemblers
rationale: Product assemblers assemble manufactured goods. Some assembly is automated but many tasks require manual dexterity.

title: Metal Engineering Process Workers
rationale: Metal engineering process workers assist in metal manufacturing. Physical production work with some machine operation.

title: Plastics and Rubber Factory Workers
rationale: Plastics and rubber factory workers perform physical production tasks.

title: Product Quality Controllers
rationale: Product quality controllers inspect manufactured goods. AI-powered visual inspection is advancing, but physical handling and assessment remain important.

title: Timber and Wood Process Workers
rationale: Timber and wood process workers perform physical wood processing tasks.

title: Other Factory Process Workers
rationale: Other factory process workers perform various physical production tasks.

title: Aquaculture Workers
rationale: Aquaculture workers perform physical work with fish and aquatic stock in outdoor/water environments.

title: Crop Farm Workers
rationale: Crop farm workers perform physical agricultural labour — planting, weeding, harvesting.

title: Forestry and Logging Workers
rationale: Forestry and logging workers perform physical work in remote forest environments. Heavy manual work with strong protection.

title: Garden and Nursery Labourers
rationale: Garden and nursery labourers perform physical horticultural work — digging, planting, watering.

title: Livestock Farm Workers
rationale: Livestock farm workers perform physical animal husbandry tasks — feeding, mustering, fencing.

title: Mixed Crop and Livestock Farm Workers
rationale: Mixed crop and livestock farm workers perform varied physical agricultural labour.

title: Other Farm, Forestry and Garden Workers
rationale: Other farm, forestry, and garden workers perform physical outdoor agricultural work.

title: Fast Food Cooks
rationale: Fast food cooks prepare food quickly in commercial kitchens. Physical, fast-paced manual work.

title: Food Trades Assistants
rationale: Food trades assistants support food preparation. Physical kitchen work.

title: Kitchenhands
rationale: Kitchenhands wash dishes, clean kitchens, and assist with food preparation. Physical manual work.

title: Freight and Furniture Handlers
rationale: Freight and furniture handlers load, unload, and move heavy items. Physical manual labour.

title: Shelf Fillers
rationale: Shelf fillers stock shelves in retail environments. Physical work, though some warehouse automation is emerging.

title: Caretakers
rationale: Caretakers maintain buildings and grounds. Physical maintenance work requiring presence.

title: Deck and Fishing Hands
rationale: Deck and fishing hands work on vessels performing physical tasks in marine environments. Physically demanding outdoor work.

title: Handypersons
rationale: Handypersons perform various physical repair and maintenance tasks in buildings.

title: Motor Vehicle Parts and Accessories Fitters
rationale: Motor vehicle parts fitters install vehicle parts and accessories. Hands-on physical work.

title: Printing Assistants and Table Workers
rationale: Printing assistants operate printing machinery. Some print production is digitally streamlined, but physical machine operation remains.

title: Recycling and Rubbish Collectors
rationale: Recycling and rubbish collectors perform physical waste collection and sorting in varied outdoor conditions.

title: Vending Machine Attendants
rationale: Vending machine attendants stock and maintain vending machines. Physical routine work.

title: Other Miscellaneous Labourers
rationale: Other miscellaneous labourers perform various physical manual tasks.
